Inspired by the groundbreaking work of Stephen Wolfram, the British-American computer scientist, physicist, and entrepreneur, this series pays tribute to his visionary insights into computational processes that have profoundly shaped my artistic journey. Wolfram's theory in 'A New Kind of Science' proposes that the universe operates through simple, deterministic rules, giving rise to complex phenomena much like the patterns seen in Cellular Automata simulations. Crafted with p5js using Cellular Automata, Arduino, and Photoshop, each piece in this series delves into the intricate beauty and complexity of computational universes.

column2: Galxe Art Campaign: SCHIELE'S GHOST SUBLIME: 19#OLIVERsub
column13: Sublime is a collection that delves into the surreal, exploring the hallucinatory capabilities of generative AI as it attempts to reconstruct the essence of Egon Schiele. In this series, 60 individuals (the base is the same used for the cyanotype collection) were photographed and their likenesses manipulated through repetitive algorithmic processes in Midjourney to see if the AI could be directed towards a specific aesthetic. The focus is not on achieving perfection, but on embracing the uncanny, the unexpected, and the imperfect - elements that stand in contrast to the hyper-realistic trends dominating contemporary AI-generated art. The time-intensive process of curating these works, from prompting to selection, reflects a deep commitment to maintaining consistency while pushing the limits of what AI can produce. Challenging the viewer to rethink the role of technology in art, Sublime presents a collection that is as much about the process as the final product - a witness to the unpredictable and often uncanny aesthetic that emerges when human creativity and artificial intelligence meet.

'AI orgy' presents a complex and dynamic scene where human figures seem to mingle in a baroque composition. The characters are depicted with great attention to anatomical details, while the background is loaded with ornamental patterns and dramatic plays of light. The poses and expressions of the characters suggest intense movement and interaction, evoking an atmosphere of festivity and controlled chaos. The color palette uses warm and rich tones, creating a striking contrast with the darker elements of the work. This composition could be interpreted as an exploration of the interconnection and fusion of human entities in a world where physical boundaries are blurred. The work invites reflection on the themes of technology and humanity, while using classical references in its composition. The technique used seems to combine historical influences with a modern interpretation, which reinforces its visual impact. And the choose of this thema is the most specific human activities treated by an artificial intelligence without any humanity.

This series of abstract digital paintings delves into the intricate tapestry of cultural identity, exploring the intersection of Colombian roots and the vibrant Berlin cityscape. Inspired by the exhilarating chaos of cultural shock and the nostalgic longing for a homeland left behind, these works capture the vibrant energy of both worlds. Organic shapes reminiscent of Colombian landscapes are intertwined with bold, urban colors and textures inspired by graffiti and techno party posters, reflecting the artist's dynamic experience navigating between two distinct cultures. The collection offers a visual exploration of the emotional journey of an immigrant, grappling with the tension between tradition and modernity, belonging and alienation.

Code Green is a series of alien viruses. They accidentally find the Earth database while roaming in the universe, then they start to disguise themselves as human beings and sneak into the Earth. By using the advantages of “evolving” and “mutating” genes, they quickly learn all the Earth civilization.Code Green employs “mind-control” tactics to infiltrate humans and ultimately leading to chaos. However, there is still a gleam of hope — a traceability program. Once human employs ERC-1155 technology to trace the source of origin that caused the mayhem, they’ll capture the key to restore once glorified civilization.Each body part of Code Green has a different gene, and each gene can be strengthened separately. Code Green is constituted of 7 components: skin, head-deco, eyes, mouth, clothes, accessories, and weapon. Just like contemporary RPG games, users can suit up each gene as they wish. These genes have the chance to mutate and evolve in future game scenes and may trigger extra gene upgrading under certain circumstances.Code Green can initiate mutation or upgrade for each component with the consumption of meta-energy, thus enhancing the attributes of a specific component. The offspring can inherit the mutated components through reproduction. Users can strengthen different components to trigger various and surprising battle combos.

Inspired by the groundbreaking work of Stephen Wolfram, the British-American computer scientist, physicist, and entrepreneur, this series pays tribute to his visionary insights into computational processes that have profoundly shaped my artistic journey. Wolfram's theory in 'A New Kind of Science' proposes that the universe operates through simple, deterministic rules, giving rise to complex phenomena much like the patterns seen in Cellular Automata simulations. Crafted with p5js using Cellular Automata, Arduino, and Photoshop, each piece in this series delves into the intricate beauty and complexity of computational universes.
